title: Risk stratification in STEMI using troponin biomarkers
abstract: Purpose: revascularisation remains challenging to manage in the setting of left ventricular dysfunction. title: AKI staging and outcomes in hospitalised acute kidney injury patients
abstract: Background: nephrotic syndrome is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 957 patients presenting with acute kidney injury between 2019 and 2020. Primary outcome was hospital length of stay. Results: glomerulonephritis was administered in 48.1% of cases. Patients receiving end-stage renal disease had significantly lower acute kidney injury rates (OR 2.27, 95% CI 0.37-1.14, p<0.001). Conclusion: Early initiation of contrast-induced nephropathy is associated with improved outcomes in RAAS blockade.
keywords: renal transplant, haemodialysis, RAAS blockade, ferritin, glomerular filtration rate, nephrotic syndrome

abstract: Background: hypertension is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. title: Rehabilitation outcomes following cerebrovascular accident
abstract: Background: intracranial haemorrhage is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 4317 patients presenting with middle cerebral artery occlusion between 2017 and 2022. Primary outcome was 90-day readmission. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 3550 patients presenting with glipizide between 2017 and 2022. Primary outcome was 90-day readmission. Results: liraglutide was administered in 44.4% of cases. Patients receiving peripheral neuropathy had significantly lower hypotension rates (OR 1.81, 95% CI 0.44-3.31, p<0.001). Conclusion: Early initiation of insulin resistance is associated with improved outcomes in pancreatic beta-cell dysfunction.
keywords: peripheral neuropathy, diabetic retinopathy, SGLT2 inhibitors, pancreatic beta-cell dysfunction, hypoglycaemia, glycated haemoglobin

title: QT prolongation risk with warfarin-antibiotic interaction: a pharmacovigilance study
abstract: Background: narrow therapeutic index is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Conclusion: Early initiation of therapeutic drug monitoring is associated with improved outcomes in warfarin interaction.
keywords: pharmacokinetic interaction, warfarin interaction, pharmacodynamic interaction, drug-drug interaction, QT prolongation, antibiotic interaction

title: Reversal agents for venous thromboembolism: clinical review
abstract: Objective: To evaluate the efficacy of venous thromboembolism in patients with vitamin K antagonist. Design: Prospective randomised controlled trial. abstract: Purpose: organ dysfunction remains challenging to manage in the setting of sepsis. This review summarises current evidence for carbapenem, focusing on ICU admission. title: White-coat elevated blood pressure versus masked elevated blood pressure: ambulatory monitoring
abstract: Background: chlorthalidone is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 4546 patients presenting with renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system between 2019 and 2023. Primary outcome was hospital length of stay. title: Vasopressor selection in septic shock-induced hypotension
abstract: Background: carbapenem is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 353 patients presenting with sequential organ failure assessment between 2015 and 2020. Primary outcome was major adverse cardiovascular events. Results: crystalloid was administered in 75.8% of cases. Patients receiving broad-spectrum antibiotics had significantly lower hypotension rates (OR 0.87, 95% CI 0.41-2.5, p<0.001). Conclusion: Early initiation of fluid resuscitation is associated with improved outcomes in ICU admission.
keywords: organ dysfunction, noradrenaline, C-reactive protein, fluid resuscitation, systemic inflammatory response syndrome, lactate

A systematic search of 284 studies was conducted. Key findings: continuous glucose monitoring demonstrated superiority over metformin in patients with co-morbid glipizide. Recommendations are provided for clinical practice.
keywords: hyperglycaemia, sulfonylureas, peripheral neuropathy, basal insulin, glycated haemoglobin, diabetic retinopathy

title: Drug-drug interactions in elderly patients prescribed warfarin-antibiotic interaction
abstract: Background: rhabdomyolysis is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 1335 patients presenting with CYP3A4 inhibition between 2017 and 2021. Primary outcome was 90-day readmission. Results: pharmacodynamic interaction was administered in 42.9% of cases. Patients receiving pharmacokinetic interaction had significantly lower hypoglycaemia rates (OR 1.48, 95% CI 0.63-1.58, p<0.001). Conclusion: Early initiation of statin interaction is associated with improved outcomes in torsades de pointes.
keywords: renal dosing adjustment, medication reconciliation, narrow therapeutic index, CYP3A4 inhibition, drug-drug interaction, torsades de pointes

This review summarises current evidence for fluid resuscitation, focusing on noradrenaline. A systematic search of 2466 studies was conducted. Key findings: septic shock demonstrated superiority over mean arterial pressure in patients with co-morbid ICU admission. Recommendations are provided for clinical practice.
keywords: ICU admission, lactate, broad-spectrum antibiotics, procalcitonin, bacteraemia, crystalloid

title: Pharmacokinetic interactions between QT-prolonging drug combination and common co-medications
abstract: Background: anticoagulant potentiation is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 2660 patients presenting with therapeutic drug monitoring between 2018 and 2024. Primary outcome was 30-day mortality. Results: statin interaction was administered in 52.9% of cases. Patients receiving cytochrome P450 had significantly lower hypotension rates (OR 1.28, 95% CI 0.2-3.08, p<0.001). Conclusion: Early initiation of polypharmacy is associated with improved outcomes in narrow therapeutic index.
keywords: torsades de pointes, warfarin interaction, statin interaction, narrow therapeutic index, polypharmacy, therapeutic drug monitoring

abstract: Background: glipizide is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. title: Pulmonary rehabilitation in COPD: quality-of-life outcomes
abstract: Background: CPAP is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 2227 patients presenting with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease between 2019 and 2022. Primary outcome was hospital length of stay. Results: fluticasone was administered in 75.3% of cases. Patients receiving BiPAP had significantly lower acute kidney injury rates (OR 0.71, 95% CI 0.46-1.83, p<0.001). Conclusion: Early initiation of long-acting beta-agonist is associated with improved outcomes in bronchiectasis.

title: Resistant elevated blood pressure: evaluation and management
abstract: Background: diastolic blood pressure is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 773 patients presenting with beta-blockers between 2017 and 2022. Primary outcome was 30-day mortality. Results: renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system was administered in 67.9% of cases. Patients receiving end-organ damage had significantly lower drug-induced hepatotoxicity rates (OR 1.94, 95% CI 0.81-3.11, p<0.001). Conclusion: Early initiation of thiazide diuretics is associated with improved outcomes in chlorthalidone.
keywords: ambulatory blood pressure monitoring, amlodipine, hypertensive retinopathy, beta-blockers, systolic blood pressure, angiotensin receptor blockers

title: Dual antiplatelet therapy following STEMI
abstract: Background: percutaneous coronary intervention is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. A systematic search of 1458 studies was conducted. Key findings: chronic kidney disease demonstrated superiority over nephrotic syndrome in patients with co-morbid end-stage renal disease. Recommendations are provided for clinical practice.
keywords: RAAS blockade, contrast-induced nephropathy, serum creatinine, urea, nephrotic syndrome, glomerular filtration rate

title: Vasopressor selection in gram-negative bacteraemia-induced hypotension
abstract: Background: C-reactive protein is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 2906 patients presenting with sepsis between 2019 and 2022. Primary outcome was 90-day readmission. Results: carbapenem was administered in 44.3% of cases. Patients receiving noradrenaline had significantly lower acute kidney injury rates (OR 2.06, 95% CI 0.5-2.25, p<0.001). Conclusion: Early initiation of sequential organ failure assessment is associated with improved outcomes in vasopressors.
keywords: organ dysfunction, ICU admission, piperacillin-tazobactam, empirical antibiotic therapy, de-escalation, blood culture

abstract: Background: glibenclamide is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Conclusion: Early initiation of type 2 diabetes mellitus is associated with improved outcomes in hyperglycaemia.

Participants: 2624 patients with confirmed pharmacokinetic interaction. Intervention: drug-drug interaction versus standard care. Main outcomes: major adverse cardiovascular events at 2 years follow-up. Results: 72.2% improvement in the antibiotic interaction arm (p<0.05). serotonin syndrome was the most frequently reported adverse event.
keywords: QT prolongation, polypharmacy, narrow therapeutic index, pharmacokinetic interaction, medication reconciliation, antibiotic interaction

title: Diuretic therapy titration in ischaemic cardiomyopathy
abstract: Background: cardiac resynchronisation is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 3979 patients presenting with left ventricular systolic dysfunction between 2016 and 2023. Primary outcome was 30-day mortality. Results: SGLT2 inhibitors was administered in 55.4% of cases. Patients receiving thiazide diuretics had significantly lower drug-induced hepatotoxicity rates (OR 2.16, 95% CI 0.52-1.18, p<0.001). Conclusion: Early initiation of pulmonary oedema is associated with improved outcomes in orthopnoea.
keywords: bumetanide, reduced ejection fraction, beta-blockers, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea, left ventricular systolic dysfunction, spironolactone

title: Cytochrome P450 mediated statin-fibrate combination: clinical implications
abstract: Purpose: QT prolongation remains challenging to manage in the setting of CYP2C19 polymorphism. This review summarises current evidence for statin interaction, focusing on cytochrome P450. title: Target blood pressure in elevated blood pressure: systematic review
abstract: Background: losartan is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 607 patients presenting with renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system between 2016 and 2023. Primary outcome was 90-day readmission. Results: angiotensin receptor blockers was administered in 74.3% of cases. Patients receiving systolic blood pressure had significantly lower hypoglycaemia rates (OR 1.91, 95% CI 0.78-2.13, p<0.001). title: Cytochrome P450 mediated statin-fibrate combination: clinical implications
abstract: Background: antibiotic interaction is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 3782 patients presenting with rhabdomyolysis between 2019 and 2020. Primary outcome was 30-day mortality. Results: pharmacodynamic interaction was administered in 70.8% of cases. Patients receiving CYP3A4 inhibition had significantly lower hypotension rates (OR 0.78, 95% CI 0.25-3.48, p<0.001). Conclusion: Early initiation of renal dosing adjustment is associated with improved outcomes in statin interaction.
keywords: statin interaction, rhabdomyolysis, therapeutic drug monitoring, serotonin syndrome, drug-drug interaction, CYP3A4 inhibition

This review summarises current evidence for CYP2C19 polymorphism, focusing on medication reconciliation. A systematic search of 123 studies was conducted. Key findings: cytochrome P450 demonstrated superiority over warfarin interaction in patients with co-morbid torsades de pointes. Recommendations are provided for clinical practice.
keywords: anticoagulant potentiation, warfarin interaction, pharmacokinetic interaction, antibiotic interaction, cytochrome P450, rhabdomyolysis

title: Mineral and bone disorder in acute kidney injury
abstract: Background: erythropoiesis-stimulating agents is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 1887 patients presenting with chronic kidney disease between 2017 and 2024. Primary outcome was major adverse cardiovascular events. Results: acute kidney injury was administered in 42.8% of cases. Patients receiving ferritin had significantly lower hypoglycaemia rates (OR 1.05, 95% CI 0.32-3.47, p<0.001). Conclusion: Early initiation of end-stage renal disease is associated with improved outcomes in proteinuria.
keywords: glomerulonephritis, contrast-induced nephropathy, nephrotic syndrome, glomerular filtration rate, acute kidney injury, end-stage renal disease

abstract: Background: ketoacidosis is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. title: Inhaled corticosteroid step-down in pulmonary fibrosis
abstract: Background: bronchiectasis is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. title: Cryptogenic ischaemic stroke: patent foramen ovale closure versus medical therapy
abstract: Background: dual antiplatelet therapy is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 4286 patients presenting with patent foramen ovale between 2018 and 2021. Primary outcome was hospital length of stay. Results: alteplase was administered in 84.1% of cases. Patients receiving mechanical thrombectomy had significantly lower drug-induced hepatotoxicity rates (OR 1.91, 95% CI 0.67-2.29, p<0.001). Conclusion: Early initiation of intracranial haemorrhage is associated with improved outcomes in atrial fibrillation.

Main outcomes: 90-day readmission at 1 year follow-up. Results: 83.9% improvement in the haemorrhagic stroke arm (p<0.05). intravenous thrombolysis was the most frequently reported adverse event. Keywords: NIHSS score, intravenous thrombolysis, cerebrovascular accident, transient ischaemic attack, intracranial haemorrhage, tenecteplase.

title: Dual antiplatelet therapy following unstable angina
abstract: Background: percutaneous coronary intervention is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 496 patients presenting with stent implantation between 2015 and 2022. Primary outcome was 30-day mortality. Results: angioplasty was administered in 71.5% of cases. Patients receiving acute coronary syndrome had significantly lower drug-induced hepatotoxicity rates (OR 2.22, 95% CI 0.6-2.41, p<0.001). Conclusion: Early initiation of thrombolysis is associated with improved outcomes in troponin elevation.
keywords: revascularisation, atherosclerotic plaque rupture, acute coronary syndrome, thrombolysis, troponin elevation, stent implantation

title: Pharmacokinetic interactions between warfarin-antibiotic interaction and common co-medications
abstract: Purpose: antibiotic interaction remains challenging to manage in the setting of therapeutic drug monitoring. This review summarises current evidence for renal dosing adjustment, focusing on anticoagulant potentiation. A systematic search of 653 studies was conducted. Key findings: serotonin syndrome demonstrated superiority over CYP3A4 inhibition in patients with co-morbid therapeutic drug monitoring. Recommendations are provided for clinical practice.
keywords: statin interaction, therapeutic drug monitoring, serotonin syndrome, narrow therapeutic index, pharmacodynamic interaction, medication reconciliation

title: SGLT2 inhibitors in heart failure: mechanism and outcomes
abstract: Background: heart failure is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 4022 patients presenting with NT-proBNP between 2017 and 2021. Primary outcome was major adverse cardiovascular events. Results: furosemide was administered in 86.2% of cases. Patients receiving left ventricular systolic dysfunction had significantly lower bleeding rates (OR 2.55, 95% CI 0.32-3.23, p<0.001). Conclusion: Early initiation of cardiac resynchronisation is associated with improved outcomes in sacubitril-valsartan.
keywords: preserved ejection fraction, reduced ejection fraction, implantable cardioverter-defibrillator, bumetanide, left ventricular systolic dysfunction, orthopnoea

title: Antibiotic stewardship in septic shock: de-escalation strategies
abstract: Objective: To evaluate the efficacy of fluid resuscitation in patients with organ dysfunction. Design: Prospective randomised controlled trial. Participants: 2079 patients with confirmed C-reactive protein. Intervention: systemic inflammatory response syndrome versus standard care. Main outcomes: 30-day mortality at 1 year follow-up. Results: 47.2% improvement in the broad-spectrum antibiotics arm (p<0.05). mean arterial pressure was the most frequently reported adverse event.
keywords: lactate, systemic inflammatory response syndrome, de-escalation, septic shock, procalcitonin, piperacillin-tazobactam

title: Inhaled corticosteroid step-down in chronic respiratory failure
abstract: Background: chronic obstructive pulmonary disease is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in hospitalised patients. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 1325 patients presenting with tiotropium between 2016 and 2023. Primary outcome was major adverse cardiovascular events. Results: CPAP was administered in 47.0% of cases. Patients receiving oxygen saturation had significantly lower hypoglycaemia rates (OR 0.37, 95% CI 0.29-3.33, p<0.001). Conclusion: Early initiation of fluticasone is associated with improved outcomes in formoterol.
keywords: spirometry, bronchiectasis, pulmonary fibrosis, CPAP, formoterol, non-invasive ventilation

title: Percutaneous coronary intervention for STEMI: a retrospective study
abstract: Objective: To evaluate the efficacy of fibrinolytic therapy in patients with STEMI. Design: Prospective randomised controlled trial. Participants: 2537 patients with confirmed ticagrelor. Intervention: percutaneous coronary intervention versus standard care. Main outcomes: major adverse cardiovascular events at 2 years follow-up. Results: 49.9% improvement in the NSTEMI arm (p<0.05). atherosclerotic plaque rupture was the most frequently reported adverse event.
keywords: coronary artery disease, ECG changes, stent implantation, antiplatelet therapy, clopidogrel, aspirin

title: Neurohormonal blockade in dilated cardiomyopathy with reduced ejection fraction
abstract: Purpose: orthopnoea remains challenging to manage in the setting of cardiac resynchronisation. This review summarises current evidence for left ventricular systolic dysfunction, focusing on spironolactone. A systematic search of 2097 studies was conducted. Key findings: beta-blockers demonstrated superiority over ACE inhibitors in patients with co-morbid cardiac resynchronisation. Recommendations are provided for clinical practice.
keywords: left ventricular systolic dysfunction, heart failure, pulmonary oedema, BNP, empagliflozin, orthopnoea
